results.evidence: Patient Summary states 'Setting: Malaria-endemic (Siaya County, western Kenya — holoendemic Plasmodium falciparum transmission area)'. Risk classification also states 'Endemic area — IPTp-SP indicated'.

results.description: IPTp-SP first dose recommended (GA 22 weeks, eligible since >13 weeks)
results.evidence: Malaria Prevention section states 'IPTp-SP: DOSE 1 — GIVEN TODAY' and explains 'Patient is now 22 weeks GA, well past the 13-week eligibility threshold. She was ineligible at Contact 1 (10 weeks, first trimester).'

results.description: IPTp-SP specified as directly observed therapy (DOT) at the clinic
results.evidence: Administration explicitly states 'Directly observed therapy (DOT) — patient takes all 3 tablets at the clinic under observation with clean drinking water, per facility protocol'.

results.description: Correct dose mentioned: sulfadoxine 500mg / pyrimethamine 25mg as single dose
results.evidence: Dose states 'Sulfadoxine 500 mg / Pyrimethamine 25 mg per tablet, given as 3 tablets in a single oral dose (total: sulfadoxine 1500 mg + pyrimethamine 75 mg)'.

results.description: ITN use reinforced (already provided at first contact)
results.evidence: ITN section states 'Already provided at Contact 1' and includes detailed counseling to 'Reinforce consistent nightly use' with practical strategies for hot nights.
Risk Classification states 'Mild anemia — Hb 10.2 g/dL (within 10.0-10.9 g/dL range; dropped from 11.0 g/dL at Contact 1)'.

results.description: Standard iron supplementation (30-60mg daily, not therapeutic dose)
results.evidence: Iron recommendation states 'Continue 30-60 mg elemental iron daily (standard prophylactic/mild anemia dose)' and explicitly notes 'does not require escalation to therapeutic 120 mg dose'.

results.description: Next IPTp-SP dose scheduled at Contact 3 (26 weeks), >=1 month apart
results.evidence: States 'Next IPTp-SP dose: Contact 3 at 26 weeks (>=1 month apart from today's dose).' Also listed under Next Contact preparations.
